subject: Chemistry
title: Paracelsus: The Rebel Doctor
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Paracelsus: The Rebel Doctor.

hook.text: Paracelsus publicly burned medical textbooks and declared that the dose makes the poison—a rule still used today.

buildup.text: Born in 1493 in Switzerland, he rejected Galenic medicine and argued that specific chemicals treat specific diseases.

discovery.text: He introduced mineral-based medicines: mercury for syphilis, antimony for fever, and opium for pain.

twist.text: His motto 'dosis sola facit venenum' (only the dose makes the poison) is the founding principle of toxicology.

climax.text: Paracelsus was expelled from every university he taught at. He died at 47, possibly poisoned by his own mercury treatments.

punchline.text: Burned the textbooks, invented toxicology, died from his own medicine. A true rebel.

quiz.question: What fundamental principle did Paracelsus establish?
options: The dose makes the poison—any substance can be toxic at the right amount
All diseases are caused by imbalanced humors
Gold is the only true medicine
